title: The Stag In The Ox Stall
content:
Safe enough lay the poor hunted Deer
    In the ox-stall, with nothing to fear
    From the careless-eyed men:
    Till the Master came; then
    There was no hiding-place for the Deer.

    An Eye Is Keen In Its Own Interest
meaning:
This evocative piece by Walter Crane, titled "The Stag In The Ox Stall", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
